evidence_quotes.quote: If the game studios want to close a game for good, they should leave the community a way to still play the game on custom-built servers.
Users paid for a good, not for a license to a good that could be killed in 15 years because of the company's whims.

paragraphs.text: [This is coming from a Software Engineer] Users paid for a good, not for a license to a good that could be killed in 15 years because of the company's whims. If the game studios want to close a game for good, they should leave the community a way to still play the game on custom-built servers. The company doesn't have to "support" the development of such servers, just to prove that one could reaosnable instantiate one such build All claims that this would be feasibly impossible or too costly are utter and complete bullshit, can't be more frank than that. The effort to setup such a server with docs is a day's worth of work and the companies ALREADY have this documentation ready. What do you think happens when one of their servers breaks down ? Do they panic becuase they don't know how to spin up another one ? NO! They already have the know-how! It's as easy as pressing a button!
